questionText: A text editor must let the user invoke 'Save', 'Print' and 'Bold' both from menu items and toolbar buttons, decoupling the widget that triggers the action from the object that performs it, and supporting macro recording. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: Encapsulating a request as a first-class object decoupled from its invoker (and enabling macros/undo) is the Command pattern.

questionText: Complete the Decorator: SlackDecorator wraps a Notifier and adds Slack.
explanation: A decorator implements the same component interface, holds a wrappee, and adds behavior around the delegated call.

questionText: Match each design pattern to the intent that best describes it.
matchingOptions: Converts the interface of a class into another interface that clients expect
Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime
Encapsulates a request as an object, allowing parameterization, queuing, and undo
Composes objects into tree structures and lets clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly
Centralizes communication between a set of objects so they no longer refer to each other directly
Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter

correctAnswers.Adapter: Converts the interface of a class into another interface that clients expect
correctAnswers.Command: Encapsulates a request as an object, allowing parameterization, queuing, and undo
correctAnswers.Composite: Composes objects into tree structures and lets clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly
correctAnswers.Mediator: Centralizes communication between a set of objects so they no longer refer to each other directly
correctAnswers.Simple Factory: Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
correctAnswers.Strategy: Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: An image gallery must avoid loading large image files until they actually become visible on screen, substituting a lightweight placeholder that loads the real image on first display. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: A virtual proxy stands in for an expensive object and creates it on demand — the (virtual) Proxy pattern.

questionText: Complete the Decorator: EncryptDecorator wraps a DataSource and adds Encrypted.
explanation: A decorator implements the same component interface, holds a wrappee, and adds behavior around the delegated call.

questionText: Group the following design patterns with their defining intent.
matchingOptions: Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
Defines a one-to-many dependency so that when one object changes state all its dependents are notified automatically
Ensures a class has only one instance and provides a global access point to it
Encapsulates a request as an object, allowing parameterization, queuing, and undo

correctAnswers.Command: Encapsulates a request as an object, allowing parameterization, queuing, and undo
correctAnswers.Observer: Defines a one-to-many dependency so that when one object changes state all its dependents are notified automatically
correctAnswers.Simple Factory: Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
correctAnswers.Singleton: Ensures a class has only one instance and provides a global access point to it

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: Which design pattern does the following code implement?
explanation: Private constructor + static field + static accessor returning the one instance is Singleton.

questionText: Complete the Command pattern wrapping Light.turnOn().
explanation: The command implements Command, stores the receiver, and calls the receiver's real method inside execute().

questionText: Match each design pattern to the structural/behavioral trait that identifies it.
matchingOptions: Elements expose accept(visitor) and call back visit(this) for double dispatch
A final base method calls abstract step methods that subclasses fill in
Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
Holds a reference to a separate implementation hierarchy chosen at construction time
Encapsulates a receiver plus an action behind an execute() method

correctAnswers.Bridge: Holds a reference to a separate implementation hierarchy chosen at construction time
correctAnswers.Command: Encapsulates a receiver plus an action behind an execute() method
correctAnswers.Mediator: Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
correctAnswers.Template Method: A final base method calls abstract step methods that subclasses fill in
correctAnswers.Visitor: Elements expose accept(visitor) and call back visit(this) for double dispatch

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Which design pattern does the following code implement?
explanation: A nested builder whose methods return this and end in build() is the Builder pattern.

questionText: Complete the Command pattern wrapping Door.open().
explanation: The command implements Command, stores the receiver, and calls the receiver's real method inside execute().

questionText: Group the following design patterns with their telltale implementation characteristic.
matchingOptions: A separate snapshot object stores state that an originator can later restore from
A creator class declares an abstract factory method that subclasses override
Splits object data into shared intrinsic state and per-context extrinsic state, often cached in a factory
Encapsulates a receiver plus an action behind an execute() method
Exposes hasNext() and next() to walk a collection's elements

correctAnswers.Command: Encapsulates a receiver plus an action behind an execute() method
correctAnswers.Factory Method: A creator class declares an abstract factory method that subclasses override
correctAnswers.Flyweight: Splits object data into shared intrinsic state and per-context extrinsic state, often cached in a factory
correctAnswers.Iterator: Exposes hasNext() and next() to walk a collection's elements
correctAnswers.Memento: A separate snapshot object stores state that an originator can later restore from

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Which design pattern does the following code implement?
explanation: Implementing the same interface, holding a wrapped instance, and adding behavior around the delegated call is Decorator.

questionText: Complete the Command pattern wrapping Television.mute().
explanation: The command implements Command, stores the receiver, and calls the receiver's real method inside execute().

questionText: For each pattern, pick the characteristic you would use to recognize it in code.
matchingOptions: Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
The context holds an algorithm interface reference that can be set/replaced at runtime
Both leaves and containers implement the same component interface and containers hold children
A final base method calls abstract step methods that subclasses fill in

correctAnswers.Composite: Both leaves and containers implement the same component interface and containers hold children
correctAnswers.Mediator: Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
correctAnswers.Strategy: The context holds an algorithm interface reference that can be set/replaced at runtime
correctAnswers.Template Method: A final base method calls abstract step methods that subclasses fill in

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Which design pattern does the following code implement?
explanation: Implementing the target interface while delegating to a wrapped adaptee with a different method name is Adapter.

questionText: Complete the Command pattern wrapping Document.save().
explanation: The command implements Command, stores the receiver, and calls the receiver's real method inside execute().

questionText: Match each design pattern to the structural/behavioral trait that identifies it.
matchingOptions: Holds a reference to a separate implementation hierarchy chosen at construction time
Each handler holds a reference to the next handler and forwards what it cannot handle
Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
Uses a fluent chain of setter-style methods ending in a build() call
A separate snapshot object stores state that an originator can later restore from
Splits object data into shared intrinsic state and per-context extrinsic state, often cached in a factory

correctAnswers.Bridge: Holds a reference to a separate implementation hierarchy chosen at construction time
correctAnswers.Builder: Uses a fluent chain of setter-style methods ending in a build() call
correctAnswers.Chain of Responsibility: Each handler holds a reference to the next handler and forwards what it cannot handle
correctAnswers.Flyweight: Splits object data into shared intrinsic state and per-context extrinsic state, often cached in a factory
correctAnswers.Mediator: Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
correctAnswers.Memento: A separate snapshot object stores state that an originator can later restore from

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Which design pattern does the following code implement?
explanation: Maintaining a subscriber list and calling update() on each is the Observer pattern.

questionText: Complete the Command pattern wrapping Robot.moveForward().
explanation: The command implements Command, stores the receiver, and calls the receiver's real method inside execute().

questionText: Group the following design patterns with their telltale implementation characteristic.
matchingOptions: A separate snapshot object stores state that an originator can later restore from
Both leaves and containers implement the same component interface and containers hold children
Exposes hasNext() and next() to walk a collection's elements
A creator class declares an abstract factory method that subclasses override
Elements expose accept(visitor) and call back visit(this) for double dispatch

correctAnswers.Composite: Both leaves and containers implement the same component interface and containers hold children

correctAnswers.Memento: A separate snapshot object stores state that an originator can later restore from

questionText: Which design pattern does the following code implement?
explanation: A final method that calls abstract steps subclasses fill in is Template Method.

questionText: Complete the Template Method skeleton in DataProcessor.
explanation: The template method is final so subclasses cannot change the step order; the steps are abstract for subclasses to implement.

questionText: For each pattern, pick the characteristic you would use to recognize it in code.
matchingOptions: A wrapper that implements the same interface as the object it wraps and adds behavior around it
A creator class declares an abstract factory method that subclasses override
Wraps an existing class and translates its calls to a target interface
Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
Exposes hasNext() and next() to walk a collection's elements

correctAnswers.Adapter: Wraps an existing class and translates its calls to a target interface
correctAnswers.Decorator: A wrapper that implements the same interface as the object it wraps and adds behavior around it
correctAnswers.Factory Method: A creator class declares an abstract factory method that subclasses override
correctAnswers.Iterator: Exposes hasNext() and next() to walk a collection's elements
correctAnswers.Mediator: Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Which design pattern does the following code implement?
explanation: Holding a replaceable algorithm interface and delegating to it at runtime is Strategy.

questionText: Complete the Template Method skeleton in Game.
explanation: The template method is final so subclasses cannot change the step order; the steps are abstract for subclasses to implement.

questionText: Match each design pattern to the structural/behavioral trait that identifies it.
matchingOptions: A wrapper that implements the same interface as the object it wraps and adds behavior around it
A creator class declares an abstract factory method that subclasses override
Relies on a clone()/copy operation to duplicate a pre-configured object
Uses a private constructor and a static accessor so only one object ever exists
Each handler holds a reference to the next handler and forwards what it cannot handle
Wraps an existing class and translates its calls to a target interface

correctAnswers.Adapter: Wraps an existing class and translates its calls to a target interface
correctAnswers.Chain of Responsibility: Each handler holds a reference to the next handler and forwards what it cannot handle
correctAnswers.Decorator: A wrapper that implements the same interface as the object it wraps and adds behavior around it
correctAnswers.Factory Method: A creator class declares an abstract factory method that subclasses override
correctAnswers.Prototype: Relies on a clone()/copy operation to duplicate a pre-configured object
correctAnswers.Singleton: Uses a private constructor and a static accessor so only one object ever exists

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Which design pattern does the following code implement?
explanation: Wrapping a receiver and its action behind execute() is the Command pattern.

questionText: Complete the Template Method skeleton in ReportGenerator.

questionText: Group the following design patterns with their telltale implementation characteristic.
matchingOptions: Relies on a clone()/copy operation to duplicate a pre-configured object
An object implementing the same interface as the real subject intercepts calls to it

correctAnswers.Prototype: Relies on a clone()/copy operation to duplicate a pre-configured object

questionText: Match each design pattern to the structural/behavioral trait that identifies it.
matchingOptions: Splits object data into shared intrinsic state and per-context extrinsic state, often cached in a factory
A single class delegates to several subsystem classes to hide their complexity
Holds a reference to a separate implementation hierarchy chosen at construction time
An object implementing the same interface as the real subject intercepts calls to it
A final base method calls abstract step methods that subclasses fill in

correctAnswers.Proxy: An object implementing the same interface as the real subject intercepts calls to it
correctAnswers.Template Method: A final base method calls abstract step methods that subclasses fill in

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Which design pattern does the following code implement?
explanation: A container that implements the same interface as its parts and recurses over children is Composite.

questionText: Complete the State pattern: TrafficLight delegates behavior to its current TrafficLightState.
explanation: The context holds a state object, can replace it, and forwards requests to it (passing itself for transitions).

questionText: Group the following design patterns with their telltale implementation characteristic.
matchingOptions: A factory interface declares one creation method per product in a related family
Splits object data into shared intrinsic state and per-context extrinsic state, often cached in a factory
Uses a private constructor and a static accessor so only one object ever exists
The context delegates behavior to a swappable state object that it can replace at runtime

correctAnswers.Abstract Factory: A factory interface declares one creation method per product in a related family
correctAnswers.Flyweight: Splits object data into shared intrinsic state and per-context extrinsic state, often cached in a factory
correctAnswers.Singleton: Uses a private constructor and a static accessor so only one object ever exists
correctAnswers.State: The context delegates behavior to a swappable state object that it can replace at runtime

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.
